events.title: Repo rate cut 50bps to 8.50%
events.summary: Bangladesh Bank cut the repo rate by 50bps citing declining inflation trajectory and adequate banking sector liquidity. This was the second consecutive cut in the easing cycle.

events.title: Repo rate cut 50bps to 9.00% — easing cycle begins
events.summary: BB initiated the easing cycle with a 50bps repo rate cut after holding at 9.50% for nearly a year. CPI had moderated to single digits and excess liquidity was building in the banking system.

events.title: Reverse repo (SDF) rate cut 50bps to 7.00%
events.summary: The SDF rate was reduced in tandem with the repo rate cut to maintain the 200bps policy corridor width.

events.title: January 2025 MPS — Rates held, cautious stance
events.summary: BB held all policy rates steady in the January MPS, citing persistent inflationary pressures despite some moderation. Signaled readiness to ease if inflation continued to decline.

events.title: Repo rate hiked 100bps to 9.50%
events.summary: Aggressive 100bps hike to combat double-digit inflation. This completed the tightening cycle that started in mid-2023, bringing the repo rate to its highest level since 2012.

events.title: CRR maintained at 4.00%
events.summary: BB kept CRR unchanged despite tightening the policy rate, choosing to manage liquidity primarily through the rate corridor rather than reserve requirements.
